name: Upgradeable Vehicle
description: Upgradeable Vehicle is a controllable transport mechanic with dedicated movement, physics, resources, or damage behavior.
design_notes: Upgradeable Vehicle should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For vehicles mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use upgradeable vehicle as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect upgradeable vehicle to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels mastery over a machine with distinct weight, speed, and handling.

parameters.name: max_speed
parameters.description: Maximum velocity the vehicle can reach.

parameters.name: handling_response
parameters.description: How sharply steering or control input affects the vehicle.

parameters.name: traction
parameters.description: Grip coefficient used for braking, turning, or drifting.

edge_cases: Upgradeable Vehicle is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Upgradeable Vehicle overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Upgradeable Vehicle runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Upgradeable Vehicle state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Upgradeable Vehicle plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Upgradeable Vehicle produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Upgradeable Vehicle strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Upgradeable Vehicle becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Upgradeable Vehicle with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Upgradeable Vehicle as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Upgradeable Vehicle with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Upgradeable Vehicle as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
